g.data-narration: Welcome to Developing Use Cases. Learn how to capture functional requirements from user perspectives.
A use case describes how users interact with a system to achieve specific goals, capturing requirements from the actor's viewpoint.
g.text: A description of how users interact with a system to accomplish
specific goals. It captures functional requirements from the
actor's perspective, focusing on VALUE delivered to users.

g.data-narration: Use cases bridge stakeholder communication, guide testing, define system boundaries, and support iterative development.
Every use case has essential components: name, actors, preconditions, postconditions, basic flow, and alternative flows.
g.text: Key Components of a Use Case

g.data-narration: Actors are external entities interacting with the system - users, other systems, or devices. Use case names should be verb phrases.
Preconditions must be true before the use case starts. Postconditions guarantee what will be true after successful completion.
g.text: PRECONDITIONS: What must be TRUE before use case starts
POSTCONDITIONS: What is guaranteed AFTER completion

g.data-narration: Basic flow is the main success path. Alternative flows handle variations, errors, and exceptional conditions during execution.
g.text: "Customer inserts card → enters PIN → gets cash"

g.data-narration: Three types exist: Brief (one paragraph), Casual (narrative with alternatives), and Fully Dressed (complete with all components).
Include relationship represents mandatory, reusable behavior. The including use case depends on the included use case.
g.text: Mandatory inclusion of reusable behavior - ALWAYS executed

g.data-narration: Extend adds optional or exceptional behavior to a base use case under specific conditions. It's conditional and situational.
g.text: Conditional addition of optional/exceptional behavior - under specific circumstances

g.data-narration: Use case diagrams show system boundary (rectangle), actors (stick figures), use cases (ellipses), and their relationships.
Here's a complete ATM withdrawal example showing all components: actor, preconditions, basic flow steps, and alternative flows.
g.text: Precondition: Valid card, correct PIN, sufficient balance
Invalid card → eject | Invalid PIN → retain card | Insufficient balance → suggest amount

g.data-narration: Remember: Include is mandatory and reusable across use cases. Extend adds optional behavior conditionally.
g.text: Include: Mandatory, always executed, reusable | Extend: Optional, conditional, exceptional behavior

g.data-narration: Use cases capture requirements from user perspective, focusing on goals. Master include/extend relationships and diagram notation for exams.
g.text: ✓ Focus on user goals | ✓ Use <> for mandatory | ✓ <> for optional | ✓ Actors outside boundary | ✓ Pre/Post conditions define scope
